# CBD Calendar of Activities and Actions - Summary

## Background
• COP16/25 decision requests Executive Secretary to provide **"calendar of activities and actions"** to national focal points annually (starting 2024)
• Purpose: rationalize intersessional activities and facilitate workflow management
• Current SCBD calendar only shows meetings - need to add activities and actions

## Key Definitions

### Activities (by Secretariat)
• Meetings/Workshops/Expert Groups
• Compilation and analysis of information
• Documentation/Peer-review processes

### Actions (by Parties - with deadlines)
• Nominations
• Participation in meetings/workshops/expert groups
• Submission of information
• Submission of peer-review comments
• Expression of Interest (EOI)

## Analysis of Other Conventions
• UNEP, UNFCCC, UNCCD calendars focus on **events/meetings only**
• **Do NOT include activities and actions** in their design
• Use filters for event type/theme/topic
• Task of managing activities is not within their scope

## Technical Requirements

### Metadata Structure Needed
• **Start Date** (Q1 = Jan 1?, etc.)
• **End Date** (Q1 = March 30?, etc.)
• **Title**
• **Summary/Description**
• **Status** (needs definition for activities vs meetings)
• **Subject Area** (needs review/definition)
• **Activity/Action Type**
• **Linked Subsidiary Body** (SBSTTA, COP, etc.)
• **Linked Notifications**
• **Linked Decisions**

### Action Types Identified
• Nominations/registrations
• Submission of information
• Submission of peer-review comments
• Expression of Interest (EOI)

## Three Proposed Calendar Formats

### Format 1: Separated Lists
• **Separate sections** for:
  - Meetings (existing)
  - Activities (new)
  - Actions (new)
• Same filters apply to all sections
• Meetings and non-meeting items kept distinct

### Format 2: Separated + Subsidiary Body Filter
• Same as Format 1
• **Additional filter by Subsidiary Body** (SBSTTA, COP, etc.)
• Allows users to focus on specific body's activities

### Format 3: Combined/Merged View
• **Single integrated list** of meetings, activities, and actions
• Items sorted chronologically
• Color-coded by type (Meeting/Activity/Action)
• May include notifications in the list
• Most comprehensive but potentially complex

## Current Challenges

### Data Management Issues
• **Information incomplete** - missing subject areas, statuses, dates
• Need to define and standardize:
  - Status categories for activities vs meetings
  - Subject area classifications
  - Granularity level for activities

### Organizational Questions
• **Who will maintain** this information? (Critical - "Garbage in > Garbage out")
• Consistency needed between Divisions/Subsidiary Bodies
• Should Protocol activities be included?
• Notification system integration needed

### Resource Requirements
• Staff time allocation needed for data entry/maintenance
• Financial resources required for system development
• Need user interface for information management

## Next Steps Required

### From Divisions
• **SSSF, IMS & 8J & Protocols** must provide complete lists
• Fill out activity and action tables with all required metadata
• Ensure consistent tagging across divisions

### From COP Bureau
• Choose **preferred format** (1, 2, or 3)
• Decide on **level of detail** for listings
• Approve **filtering functionalities**:
  - Types of meetings, activities, actions?
  - Deadlines for actions?
  - Related subsidiary bodies/COPs?
  - Subject areas?

### Key Decision Point
• **Re-evaluate if added value justifies effort** to make activities searchable/filterable
• Consider maintenance burden vs user benefit

## Implementation Dependencies
• Bureau guidance on format and functionality
• Resource allocation (staff time + budget)
• Data standardization across all divisions
• System development and user interface creation

---

*"Once preferences by COP Bureau are identified, the Secretariat will make efforts in allocating staff time and identifying necessary financial resources"*
